                                         Parcel Information
                                
                            
                            
                                
                                    
		Parcel Number:14 00700006018 *Block:--Lot:--Subdivision:--

This tree is in direct structural conflict with the home due to its extreme proximity to the foundation. At approximately 3 feet from the structure, the
trunk and structural root system are within the foundation bearing zone. As the tree continues to grow, radial trunk expansion and increasing root
diameter will exert pressure on the foundation wall and footing, leading to cracking, displacement, and long-term structural damage. Excavation, root pruning, or installation of root barriers on the foundation side is not feasible without severing major structural roots. Removal of
these roots would significantly compromise stability and create an elevated failure risk toward the residence. Crown reduction does not mitigate the
root/foundation conflict. Given the tree’s size, maturity, and location, there is no reasonable arboricultural mitigation that would preserve the tree while protecting the
structure.

Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Failed and dead leaders present with ripped-off leader sections exhibiting visible decay. Fruiting bodies noted at the base. Overall canopy decline observed.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Magnolia Tree Size (DBH):34Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:The tree’s basal structure suggests it was cut at the base and regenerated as a stump sprout. Evidence of previous topping events observed. Loss of apical dominance occurred twice, initially when the tree was cut and regenerated as a stump sprout, and subsequently during a canopy topping event. Additional stems measure 13.3 inches and 17.1 inches DBH and were not included in the primary size calculation. Size reflects the three largest dominant trunks combined.
		
		
			Tree number:3Species:Magnolia Tree Size (DBH):54Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Structural configuration is consistent with stump sprout origin. The tree has been cut, regenerated as a stump sprout, and subsequently topped during later canopy reduction events. Two stems are grafted together. Canopy thinning noted toward the house-facing side. Additional stems measure 15.9 inches, 14.2 inches, and 12.5 inches DBH and were not included in the primary size calculation. Size reflects the three largest dominant trunks combined (19.8, 18.5, and 16.5 inches).
		
		
			Tree number:5Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):21Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Abrasion wound from adjacent tree contact. Crown congestion contributing to repeated mechanical friction.
